label: Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection

node.label: Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection
node.definition: Best-of-N and verifier-guided selection generate multiple candidates and choose one with an outcome, process, preference, test, or learned quality scorer.
node.why_it_matters: It converts sampling diversity into accuracy gains when the selector is more reliable than any single generation.
limitations: Selection can overfit verifier quirks, consume substantial compute, and discard diverse correct solutions.

dependencies.rationale: Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from Self-consistency & best-of-N inference.
Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from Outcome verifiers & executable rewards.
Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from Process supervision & step-level reward models.
Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from Test-time compute scaling.
